Test Description: Validate GPIO Group0 pins 8-39 negative-edge interrupt enable and handling by enabling INTR1 per pin, generating a falling edge via writes to 0xA0243ffc, and verifying status/clear within Default_IRQHandler.
Remarks: Build-time GPIO0/GPIO1 macro controls which GIC IRQ (87/88) and LSS_SYSREG interrupt are enabled; uses extern int_pend; stimulus via address 0xA0243ffc.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Runtime flow:
1) Entry: test_case() is called; test_err = 0.
2) If GPIO0 is defined: GIC_EnableIRQ(87). If GPIO1 is defined: GIC_EnableIRQ(88).
3) If GPIO0 is defined: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR). If GPIO1 is defined: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR).
4) Drive all pads high: write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xffffffff).
5) For i = 0..31 (covering GPIO_8..GPIO_39):
   5.1) addr1 = MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i * 4).
   5.2) write_reg(addr1, (1u << 20) | (1u << 18) | (1u << 16)).
   5.3) wait_on(10).
6) For i = 0..31 (per pin test):
   6.1) wr_val = (1u << i).
   6.2) write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, wr_val) to clear any pending raw interrupt.
   6.3) write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, wr_val) to enable INTR1 for this pin.
   6.4) wait_on(10).
   6.5) int_pend = 1.
   6.6) Generate high level on all pins: write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xffffffff).
   6.7) wait_on(30).
   6.8) Generate falling edge on this pin only: write_reg(0xA0243ffc, ~wr_val).
   6.9) Set timeout = 5000; while (int_pend && timeout--) wait_on(10).
   6.10) If timeout == 0: printf timeout error; test_err++.
7) Call finish(test_err).

Interrupt handling (asynchronously when interrupt occurs):
I1) Default_IRQHandler() executes automatically on interrupt.
I2) local_wr = (1u << i); int_pend = 0.
I3) write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xffffffff) to drive back high.
I4) raddr = MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i * 4); rdata = read_reg(raddr).
I5) If ((rdata & 0x1) != 0) then test_err++.
I6) If ((rdata & 0x2) != 0x0) then:
    - rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1).
    - If ((rdata_grp & local_wr) == 0) test_err++.
    - raddr2 = MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i * 4); write_reg(raddr2, (1u << 20) | (1u << 16)).
    - write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, local_wr).
    - rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); if (rdata_grp != 0x0) test_err++.
    - If GPIO0: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR); GIC_ClearIRQ(87).
    - If GPIO1: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR); GIC_ClearIRQ(88).
I7) Else (if ((rdata & 0x2) == 0x0)): test_err++.
column17:
- No timeouts should occur while waiting for negedge interrupt for any i in 0..31 (int_pend must be cleared by ISR before timeout reaches 0).
- In ISR: (rdata & 0x1) must equal 0; (rdata & 0x2) must be non-zero.
- In ISR: (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 & local_wr) must be non-zero before clear, and must read 0x0 after write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, local_wr).
- Overall: test_err must remain 0 at finish(test_err).
column18:
- No explicit ISR registration in this file; relies on Default_IRQHandler being active in the test framework.
- No cleanup to disable GPIO interrupts after test completion.
- Does not verify that non-target pins remain quiescent during each iteration.
- Does not explicitly validate interrupt polarity configuration beyond behavior-driven check.
